blocks.text: Ciao ciao. Today was the first day my brain stopped wanting to say hello in Greek to people as I rode on my bike. Now it's Ciao all the way.

Currently writing this one in a coffee shop in a mountain village on the 29th of June. I saw this while I was biking through central Italy from the top of a mountain, and I changed my plans to go visit. It is a little little town on the top of a hill. The town would be a perfect place to play a huge game of king of the hill.
I have never seen a village like this before.
On the the 28th. A day in Matera.
I woke up with the incredible view of the city. This time by day instead of by night.
I biked to the city early in the morning.
I was so confused. The crazy rock city I saw just looked completely normal when I biked through the streets. I was completely unimpressed.
I was pretty tired too so I just went and sat down at a coffee shop to relax, read, and wrote for all the morning.
I then decided to play tourist and did something unbelievable.
I paid for a guided walking tour.
While I waited for the tour to start I walked around and got ice cream.
I was too tired to find all the cool spots on my own and all the locals recommended that I do a tour.

Once the tour started I pretty much instantly regretted it haha. I was unable to understand much of the Italian guide. I enjoyed listing to Italian for two hours but I probably would have enjoyed the English version of the tour more.

I just pretended to understand the whole time and laughed when everyone else laughed. Then I would do the classic si si (yes yes), Or when I finally understood something I would repeat it back so it sounded like I understood what was going on.
When I tour ended I saw a lot of cool things in Matera. It's a beautiful city. I should have just walked around aimlessly all day in the old part of the town. Lesson learned. No more paid guides.

I have realizes on this trip so far that my three favourite things are meeting people, eating food, and great views. Im not a big fan of just seeing things that are historically important. It just doesn't seem to excite me.

After this I left the city of Matera. Wow it was incredible riding down from the hilltop town. At this point it was 9 pm and the sun had set. So I went down the hill fast when I had light. Then I biked on the highway in the darks till I found a trail off the road, and set up my tend in a large open field and slept the night.
Reflection on Matera.

Beautiful unique city, but i think the fact that I had, for the first time, really high expectations (especially after the view) that I was let down a little bit. I was also coming down on this high point from the day before so it was hard to top it.
Take care,
Erik
